meta.title: ICS Protocol Debate: Is Wrap It In TLS The Solution?
meta.description:
Modbus, CIP EtherNet/IP and other ICS protocols are adding security by wrapping the existing protocol in TLS. And this trend seems to be expanding. Is it the right solution for the ICS user community? The ICS vendor community?

David Smith of Schneider Electric argues for the use of TLS. He focuses on ease of development, implementation, and acceptance as well leveraging the security communities existing experience with this protocol and code bases.

Adam Crain of Automatak argues the Con side and makes the case that wrap it TLS expands the attack surface, forces encryption and does not provide key functions necessary.

The best parts are the opening statements at 1:50 and the Q&A at 25:45
